behaviors.description: The keyword-frequency analyzer assigns the correct sentiment when the review contains only words from the configured positive and negative word lists.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is local scoring logic (token counting + threshold comparison) inside one analyzer. MOCK-MISS: no module handoff is required to trigger the bug. REAL-INFRA/MOCK-HIDE: no OS/network/subprocess dependency. LLM-DEP: none; this behavior is deterministic keyword logic. REJECTION-GATE: rejected Integration because behavior text is only about the keyword-frequency analyzer’s assignment rule, not cross-module wiring.
behaviors.test_description: Call the sentiment scoring function directly with controlled review text and in-memory positive/negative lists, and assert expected label for positive-only, negative-only, and neutral threshold-edge cases.

behaviors.description: The explanation analyzer produces reasoning that references specific phrases from the original review, not generic boilerplate.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on generated explanation content quality (specific grounding vs generic text). LLM-DEP applies: a stubbed model can always be made to pass and would hide real grounding failures. MULTI-STEP does not apply; this is one agentic generation check, not a multi-agent pipeline.
behaviors.test_description: Run the real explanation analyzer on reviews containing distinctive phrases, then assert the explanation quotes or clearly references those phrases and fails if output is generic boilerplate across diverse inputs.

behaviors.description: When a review contains mixed signals (both positive and negative keywords), the sentiment score reflects the configured tie-breaking rule.
behaviors.justification: This is deterministic policy logic inside the keyword analyzer (counts + tie-break config). MOCK-MISS: failure appears in local decision logic, not module interaction. REAL-INFRA and LLM-DEP are not required. REJECTION-GATE: rejected Integration because behavior text targets configured tie-breaking in the scorer itself.
behaviors.test_description: Invoke the tie-break decision function with equal/mixed keyword counts under each configured tie-break mode and assert the selected sentiment exactly matches configuration.

behaviors.description: The explanation for a sarcastic review (e.g., "Great, another broken feature") correctly identifies the negative intent despite positive surface words.
behaviors.justification: This behavior tests semantic interpretation quality (sarcasm detection), which depends on real model judgment. LLM-DEP is direct; deterministic fixtures would hide failures. MULTI-STEP not required because it is a single explanation-generation decision.
behaviors.test_description: Evaluate the real explanation analyzer on a sarcasm set where literal polarity conflicts with intent, and assert outputs explicitly identify negative sentiment drivers rather than positive surface-word interpretations.

behaviors.description: Both outputs (score and explanation) are returned together in a single response, with the explanation referencing the same sentiment direction as the score.
behaviors.justification: This is cross-component consistency where one side is model-generated text. Integration alone can verify packaging, but not real directional alignment when explanation content comes from a real model. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: (b) yes, correctness depends on real AI output direction, so upgrade to Agentic. REJECTION-GATE: rejected Workflow because behavior text requires one model output aligned to one score, not multiple agentic decisions in sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Call the full service with real explanation generation enabled, assert response includes both fields in one payload, and validate explanation sentiment direction matches returned score across representative positive/negative/mixed inputs.
